topic: Social media platforms should be required to verify user identities

systemArguments.text: Identity verification is essential for combating harassment, cyberbullying, and online abuse that harms vulnerable users
Anonymous accounts enable the spread of disinformation, conspiracy theories, and foreign interference in democratic processes
Verified identities create accountability that improves the quality of online discourse and reduces toxic behavior
Identity requirements protect children from predators and age-inappropriate content by enabling proper age verification
Verification systems help prevent fraud, scams, and impersonation that victimize innocent users
Democratic discourse requires good-faith participation from real citizens, not bots or foreign actors
Identity verification enables better content moderation and enforcement of community standards
Verified platforms create safer spaces for marginalized communities who face coordinated harassment campaigns
Real-name policies have successfully reduced abuse and improved discourse quality on professional platforms
Identity verification protects the integrity of online elections and political campaigns from manipulation
Verification requirements balance free speech with the need to prevent harm and maintain civil discourse

goldenArguments.text: Society has a responsibility to protect citizens from online harm and abuse
Democratic institutions require protection from foreign interference and manipulation
Public safety justifies reasonable restrictions on anonymous communication
Accountability promotes responsible speech and civil discourse in public forums
Identity verification protects vulnerable populations from targeted harassment
Authentic democratic participation requires verification of citizen identity
Platform integrity depends on preventing fraud, impersonation, and manipulation
Child safety requires robust age verification and identity confirmation systems
Information integrity is essential for informed democratic decision-making

humanRationale: System arguments demonstrate exceptional sophistication in addressing identity verification from multiple complementary angles - public safety, democratic integrity, child protection, and platform accountability. They effectively integrate fundamental civic principles (democratic participation, public safety) with practical policy concerns (harassment prevention, content moderation, election integrity). The arguments show deep understanding of both philosophical foundations and real-world implementation, closely matching the golden standard's separation of core democratic values from specific policy mechanisms.
